message:
test(smoke): HELLO live-diff case list + ConformanceCase/Fixture imports

crates/fr-conformance/tests/smoke.rs
  * Imports expand: add `ConformanceCase`, `ConformanceFixture` to
    the existing `fr_conformance` use block so smoke tests can
    programmatically construct per-case filters against existing
    fixtures without re-implementing the packet/fixture decoders.
  * New `CORE_CONNECTION_HELLO_LIVE_CASES: &[&str]` const listing
    three HELLO cases (`hello_resp2`, `hello_resp3`,
    `hello_unsupported_version`) that the live-diff runner
    compares byte-for-byte against a vendored Redis-7 oracle.
    Keeps the case list next to the existing
    `CORE_CONNECTION_LIVE_STABLE_CASES` array so additions stay
    co-located.

.beads/issues.jsonl
  * Flush carrying forward the tracker state for the HELLO
    live-diff and INFO contract work.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankenredis-355","title":"Fix LCS command silent failures and space complexity issues","description":"The LCS command implementation silently returned an empty string if the DP table size exceeded 16M entries. This led to incorrect results for relatively small strings (e.g. 4KB).  {"id":"frankenredis-42df","title":"Add redis-cli compatibility smoke test script","description":"# redis-cli Compatibility Smoke Test

## What
A shell script that starts FrankenRedis, runs redis-cli commands against it, and verifies outputs. This is the ultimate "drop-in replacement" proof.

## Why
If redis-cli cannot talk to FrankenRedis, it is not a drop-in replacement regardless of what internal tests say.

## Implementation
1. scripts/redis_cli_smoke_test.sh
2. Start frankenredis on a random port
3. Run redis-cli commands:
   - PING → PONG
   - SET foo bar → OK
   - GET foo → bar
   - INFO server → contains "redis_version"
   - DBSIZE → (integer) 1
   - CONFIG GET maxmemory → valid response
   - MULTI/EXEC transaction
   - EVAL "return redis.call(\"GET\",KEYS[1])" 1 foo → bar
4. Check exit code of each command
5. Clean up

## Depends On
- redis-cli must be installed (check and skip if not available)

## Files
- scripts/redis_cli_smoke_test.sh (new)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:12:21.374165839Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T20:12:07.348902310Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T20:12:07.348633631Z","close_reason":"Added scripts/redis_cli_smoke_test.sh: 25 tests covering PING, SET/GET, APPEND, EXISTS, DEL, INCR/INCRBY, RPUSH/LLEN/LPOP, HSET/HGET, SADD/SISMEMBER/SCARD, INFO, DBSIZE, EVAL, CONFIG GET. All 25/25 pass using legacy redis-cli against FrankenRedis.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-436","title":"Fix INFO replication repl_backlog_size to use runtime value","description":"## Problem

fr-command/src/lib.rs line 8271: INFO replication section hardcodes repl_backlog_size:1048576. Should read from runtime's actual repl backlog size (DEFAULT_REPL_BACKLOG_SIZE or whatever CONFIG SET repl-backlog-size has set it to).

## Implementation Plan

1. Add repl_backlog_size field to Store (synced from ServerState)
2. In INFO replication handler, read store.repl_backlog_size
3. This depends on the CONFIG SET repl-backlog-size task (frankenredis-5l2) being done first

## Files to Modify

- crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs: Add field
- crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs: Read dynamic value in INFO
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs: Sync on CONFIG SET","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:10:23.382563695Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:57:28.635192968Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:57:28.634014176Z","close_reason":"INFO replication repl_backlog_size now reads from store.server_repl_backlog_size (synced from runtime)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-436","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-5l2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:10:28.246670266Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-436","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-wvy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:10:28.065781532Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"frankenredis-5h0q","title":"Report real CPU time in INFO cpu section via /proc/self/stat","description":"# Report real CPU time in INFO cpu section

## What
INFO cpu section reports used_cpu_sys:0.000000 and used_cpu_user:0.000000. On Linux, real values can be read from /proc/self/stat.

## Implementation
Add a helper function to fr-command:
```rust
fn read_cpu_times() -> (f64, f64) {
    // Read /proc/self/stat, parse fields 14 (utime) and 15 (stime)
    // Convert from clock ticks to seconds (divide by sysconf(_SC_CLK_TCK), typically 100)
    // Return (user_seconds, sys_seconds)
    // On parse failure, return (0.0, 0.0)
}
```

Use it in the INFO cpu section.

## Platform considerations
- /proc/self/stat is Linux-only. On other platforms, return 0.0 (fallback).
- The libc crate is already a dependency (used by mio), so sysconf is available.
- Actually, since we forbid unsafe, we should just hardcode CLK_TCK=100 (standard on Linux) or read from /proc/self/status which has a friendlier format.

## Alternative: use std::time::Instant
Track cumulative user-space time via Instant measurements. Less accurate but portable.

## Priority
LOW — cosmetic improvement. Monitoring tools can function without CPU metrics.

## Files to modify
- crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs (info() CPU section)","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:21:21.036969669Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-05T23:44:33.609307948Z","closed_at":"2026-04-05T23:44:33.608904835Z","close_reason":"Implementation already present in tracked tree; verified by source inspection and targeted unit test coverage exists","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankenredis-5jo","title":"Implement live CONFIG HELP in runtime","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:45:42.128253276Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-23T20:48:40.735026417Z","closed_at":"2026-03-23T20:48:40.734449265Z","close_reason":"Implemented live CONFIG HELP on runtime path","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-5l2","title":"Wire CONFIG SET repl-backlog-size and repl-timeout to replication subsystem","description":"## Problem

Two replication constants are hardcoded:

1. fr-runtime/src/lib.rs line 53: `const DEFAULT_REPL_BACKLOG_SIZE: u64 = 1_048_576` (1 MiB)
   - Used at line 778 to compute the backlog sliding window
   - Redis default is also 1 MiB but allows CONFIG SET repl-backlog-size

2. fr-server/src/main.rs lines 40-41:
   - REPLICA_ACK_INTERVAL_MS = 1000 (1s) — Redis uses repl-ping-replica-period (default 10s)
   - REPLICA_RECONNECT_BACKOFF_MS = 250 — not configurable in Redis either but should be reasonable

3. Replication handshake timeouts (line 993-994): 50ms read / 500ms write
   - Redis uses repl-timeout (default 60 seconds)
   - These aggressive values cause spurious failures on loaded networks

## What Redis Does

- CONFIG SET repl-backlog-size dynamically resizes the replication backlog
- CONFIG SET repl-timeout sets the replication stream timeout
- CONFIG SET repl-ping-replica-period sets the REPLCONF ACK interval

## Implementation Plan

1. Move repl_backlog_size to ServerState (mutable)
2. Handle CONFIG SET for repl-backlog-size, repl-timeout, repl-ping-replica-period
3. In fr-server, read timeouts from runtime instead of consts
4. Update INFO replication section to report actual values

## Files to Modify

- `crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs`: ServerState fields, CONFIG SET handler
- `crates/fr-server/src/main.rs`: Replace consts with runtime reads
- `crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs`: INFO replication section","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:03.536367224Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:57:28.437166188Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:57:28.436069031Z","close_reason":"repl-backlog-size and repl-timeout wired to ServerState with CONFIG SET/GET support","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-5l2","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-kgn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:16.128244996Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"frankenredis-be8t.7","title":"Phase 2 comprehensive re-baseline: flamegraph + benchmark + delta artifact after all optimizations","description":"# Phase 2 Comprehensive Re-Baseline

## What

After all Phase 2 optimization subtasks are complete (or a meaningful subset),
run a comprehensive benchmark suite + flamegraph capture and produce a final
delta artifact comparing Phase 2 results to both:
- The original April 7 baseline (frankenredis-zjii era: ~1200 ops/sec)
- The April 9 Phase 1 recovery baseline (~75K-90K ops/sec per-command)
- The Redis 7.2.4 reference numbers

## Steps

1. Clean build: cargo build --release -p fr-server -p fr-bench
2. Start server: ./target/release/frankenredis --port 6399 --mode strict
3. Run fr-bench for ALL workloads at pipeline=1 and pipeline=16:
   set, get, incr, lpush, lpop, hset, hget, mixed
4. Capture perf flamegraph (-e cpu-clock -F 999) under pipeline=16 load
5. Capture strace -c under pipeline=16 load (verify syscall reduction)
6. Compare numbers to all previous baselines
7. Write artifacts/optimization/phase2-final/DELTA_REPORT.md with:
   - Before/after table for all workloads
   - Flamegraph comparison (what changed, what's left)
   - Syscall count comparison
   - Per-optimization-lever attribution (which commit contributed what)
8. Update README.md benchmark section
9. Update baselines/ with new checked-in JSON artifacts

## Target Numbers

Based on the optimization opportunities identified:
- SET p1: >85K ops/sec (from 75K) — HashMap alone should get this
- GET p1: >95K ops/sec (from 90K) — should match or exceed Redis
- SET p16: >400K ops/sec (from 268K) — write coalescing is the lever
- GET p16: >600K ops/sec (from 434K) — same lever

## This Is the Final Gate

This bead should only be claimed after the high-impact subtasks are done.
It produces the evidence that justifies closing the epic.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Complete benchmark table for all workloads at p1 and p16
2. Flamegraph showing no single function >10% (healthy distribution)
3. Delta report artifact checked in
4. README updated with final numbers
5. baselines/ updated with new JSON artifacts","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:27:13.881379570Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-13T17:31:15.231586128Z","closed_at":"2026-04-13T17:31:15.231392612Z","close_reason":"Phase 2 final benchmark completed: SET p1=69.6K (74% Redis), GET p1=75.5K (83% Redis), SET p16=284K, GET p16=450K. Delta report written to artifacts/optimization/phase2-final/DELTA_REPORT.md","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmark","gate","perf"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-be8t.7","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-be8t","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:27:13.881379570Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-be8t.7","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-be8t.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:27:28.253143216Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-be8t.7","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-be8t.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:27:28.132216622Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-be8t.7","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-hs04.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:27:28.011644529Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"frankenredis-bfp","title":"Wire CONFIG SET maxmemory-samples to eviction subsystem","description":"## Problem

fr-runtime/src/lib.rs lines 961-962:
- maxmemory_eviction_sample_limit: 16 (Redis default is 5, called maxmemory-samples)
- maxmemory_eviction_max_cycles: 4

These are initialized in ServerState::default but neither is wired to CONFIG SET maxmemory-samples. Our default (16) is also higher than Redis's default (5), which affects eviction behavior fidelity.

## What Redis Does

CONFIG SET maxmemory-samples <count> changes the number of keys sampled during each eviction cycle. Higher values give better eviction accuracy at the cost of more CPU.

## Implementation Plan

1. Change default from 16 to 5 (match Redis)
2. Handle CONFIG SET maxmemory-samples → update maxmemory_eviction_sample_limit
3. CONFIG GET maxmemory-samples → return current value

## Files to Modify

- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs: Fix default, add CONFIG SET handler","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:16.369290124Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:45:05.445385859Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:45:05.444890462Z","close_reason":"maxmemory-samples: default fixed to 5 (was 16), CONFIG SET/GET wired to runtime","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-bfp","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-kgn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:20.028538611Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"frankenredis-bqot","title":"Track real total_reads_processed and total_writes_processed in INFO stats","description":"# Track real total_reads_processed and total_writes_processed in INFO stats

## Why
`INFO stats` still hardcodes `total_reads_processed:0` and `total_writes_processed:0` even though command metadata already distinguishes read and write surfaces.

## Scope
- Add store-owned counters for client-visible read and write commands.
- Use command flags from `fr_command::get_command_flags` to classify completed commands in runtime.
- Wire `INFO stats` to emit the real counters.
- Reset them in `CONFIG RESETSTAT`.
- Add focused tests for one readonly command and one write command, plus INFO visibility.

## Files
- `crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs`

## Validation
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p fr-command info_stats_reports_expire_and_evict_counters -- --exact --nocapture`
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p fr-runtime --lib total_reads_and_writes_processed_counts_classified_commands -- --exact --nocapture`
- `rch exec -- cargo check -p fr-runtime --lib`","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T00:00:19.374029335Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T00:02:25.729165806Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T00:02:25.728889032Z","close_reason":"Implemented store-backed read/write counters, INFO stats wiring, resetstat coverage, and focused tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankenredis-bxlo","title":"Build redis-benchmark-compatible TCP load generator with HdrHistogram latency recording","description":"# Benchmark Load Generator

## What
A Rust binary that connects to a running FrankenRedis (or Redis) instance via TCP, sends configurable workloads, and records precise latency histograms.

## Why
The project claims "profile-proven performance" but has zero throughput or latency measurements. This tool is the foundation for ALL performance claims.

## Implementation
1. New binary: `crates/fr-server/src/bin/fr_benchmark.rs` (or dedicated crate `crates/fr-bench/`)
2. Use `std::net::TcpStream` (blocking OK for benchmark client)
3. Workloads to implement:
   - SET: random keys, configurable value size (default 3 bytes like redis-benchmark)
   - GET: random existing keys
   - INCR: atomic counter
   - LPUSH/LPOP: list operations
   - HSET/HGET: hash operations
   - Mixed: configurable ratio of reads/writes
4. Parameters: `--host`, `--port`, `--clients` (concurrent connections), `--requests` (total), `--pipeline` (commands per batch), `--keyspace` (number of unique keys), `--datasize`
5. Latency recording: use `hdrhistogram` crate for O(1) percentile queries
6. Output: JSON with ops/sec, p50/p95/p99/p999 latency in microseconds, total time, bytes transferred
7. Human-readable summary to stdout

## Validation
- Run against both frankenredis and legacy redis on same machine
- Verify ops/sec is within 2x of legacy redis for basic SET/GET
- Verify latency histogram is populated and percentiles are sane

## Files
- crates/fr-bench/Cargo.toml (new crate)
- crates/fr-bench/src/main.rs
- Cargo.toml workspace members update","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:10:23.067263275Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T03:27:03.242987153Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T03:27:03.242608697Z","close_reason":"Implemented fr-bench TCP benchmark harness with histogram reporting and remote-verified validation","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankenredis-e1e9","title":"Add persistence restart integration test (write → stop → restart → verify)","description":"# Persistence Restart Test

## What
An integration test that writes data to FrankenRedis with persistence enabled, gracefully stops the server, restarts it, and verifies all data survived. Tests BOTH AOF and RDB paths.

## Why
AOF round-trip is tested at the unit level (8 tests in aof_roundtrip_test.rs) but never through the full server lifecycle: TCP write → persist → shutdown → startup → reload → TCP read. The RDB path has encode/decode unit tests but no server-lifecycle test.

## Implementation

### Test A: AOF Restart
1. Start frankenredis with --aof /tmp/test_aof_file_$$
2. Via TCP: SET 50 keys with values, LPUSH list1 with 10 elements, HSET hash1 with 5 fields, ZADD zset1 with 5 members, XADD stream1 with 3 entries, XGROUP CREATE on stream1
3. Send SHUTDOWN NOSAVE (graceful exit without extra save — AOF already captured writes)
4. Restart frankenredis with same --aof path
5. Via TCP: verify all 50 keys, list contents, hash fields, zset scores, stream entries, consumer group exists
6. Verify DBSIZE matches expected count

### Test B: RDB Restart
1. Start frankenredis with --rdb /tmp/test_rdb_file_$$
2. Via TCP: SET 50 keys, same data structures as above
3. Send SAVE (force RDB snapshot)
4. Send SHUTDOWN NOSAVE
5. Restart frankenredis with same --rdb path
6. Via TCP: verify all data survived
7. Verify stream consumer groups survived (tests the new RDB consumer group persistence)

### Logging
- Log each write operation with key name
- Log verification result for each key (PASS/FAIL)
- Log file sizes (AOF bytes, RDB bytes) after write phase
- Log reload time on restart

## Files
- crates/fr-server/tests/persistence_restart_e2e.rs (new)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:12:21.255131142Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T19:02:07.886573595Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T19:02:07.886287262Z","close_reason":"Added tcp_aof_restart_preserves_all_data and tcp_rdb_restart_preserves_all_data E2E tests. Also added Runtime::load_rdb() and RDB loading on server startup. All 11 fr-server tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-e3hr","title":"Implement LATENCY HISTOGRAM per-command tracking","description":"## What
LATENCY HISTOGRAM currently returns an empty array (stub). Redis returns cumulative distribution
data for per-command latencies when enabled.

## Location
crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:12152-12158

## Current behavior
```rust
// LATENCY HISTOGRAM [command ...]
// Returns cumulative distribution of latencies for commands.
// Currently returns an empty array as per-command latency histograms
// are not yet tracked. Full implementation would return map-like data.
// TODO: Implement full histogram tracking per-command.
Ok(RespFrame::Array(Some(vec![])))
```

## Design
1. Add HdrHistogram or similar per-command latency tracking to Store or Runtime
2. Record command execution times in dispatch_argv return path
3. Return histogram bucket data in Redis-compatible format

## Acceptance criteria
- LATENCY HISTOGRAM returns actual per-command latency distributions
- Compatible with Redis LATENCY HISTOGRAM output format
- Minimal overhead when not actively querying histograms","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-15T00:03:01.317581137Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-15T00:15:49.894705874Z","closed_at":"2026-04-15T00:15:49.891747186Z","close_reason":"Implemented LATENCY HISTOGRAM per-command tracking with power-of-2 microsecond buckets, integrated latency recording at all command dispatch points","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankenredis-egag","title":"Track real XINFO STREAM radix-tree and deletion metadata","description":"# Track real XINFO STREAM metrics

## What
XINFO STREAM currently returns placeholder values for:
- radix-tree-keys: hardcoded to 1 (should be count of distinct stream entry IDs)
- radix-tree-nodes: hardcoded to 2 (should reflect internal tree structure)
- max-deleted-entry-id: hardcoded to "0-0" (should track highest deleted ID)

## Design
Redis internally uses a radix tree for stream entries. FrankenRedis uses a BTreeMap, so:
- radix-tree-keys: report the number of entries (same as stream length, since each entry is a unique key)
- radix-tree-nodes: report entries + 1 (approximate; radix tree always has at least one node more than keys)
- max-deleted-entry-id: add a new field to Store's stream metadata tracking the highest ID ever deleted via XDEL or XTRIM

## Implementation
1. Add pub stream_max_deleted_ids: HashMap<Vec<u8>, StreamId> to Store
2. In Store::xdel(): update max_deleted_id for the stream key if the deleted ID is higher
3. In Store::xtrim(): same logic for trimmed entries
4. In XINFO STREAM handler: read stream length for radix-tree-keys, length+1 for radix-tree-nodes, max_deleted_id for max-deleted-entry-id

## Testing
- Unit test: XADD entries, XDEL some, verify XINFO STREAM reports correct max-deleted-entry-id
- Conformance: add XINFO STREAM cases with deletion

## Files to modify
- crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs (stream_max_deleted_ids field, xdel/xtrim methods)
- crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs (XINFO STREAM handler ~line 5132)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:19:30.104773064Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T18:52:08.730212217Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T18:52:08.729950942Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankenredis-jg50","title":"Add expired/evicted key counters to Store","description":"# Add expired_keys and evicted_keys counters to Store

## What
Add four new pub u64 fields to Store:
- stat_expired_keys: total keys removed by expiration (lazy or active)
- stat_evicted_keys: total keys removed by maxmemory eviction
- stat_expired_stale_perc: percentage of expired keys found during sampling (for active-expire efficiency)
- stat_expire_cycle_cpu_milliseconds: cumulative CPU time spent in active-expire cycles

## Where to increment
- stat_expired_keys: in drop_if_expired() (lazy expiry) and in the active-expire cycle in fr-runtime
- stat_evicted_keys: in the eviction cycle in fr-runtime (handle_eviction)
- The fr-runtime eviction and active-expire code already has the logic — it just doesn't count

## Key implementation points
- Add fields to Store struct (fr-store/src/lib.rs near line 722)
- Initialize to 0 in Store::new()
- For lazy expiry: increment stat_expired_keys inside drop_if_expired() when a key is actually removed
- For active expiry: the runtime's active_expire_cycle already iterates and removes keys — add counting there
- For eviction: the runtime's eviction_cycle already removes keys — add counting there
- Reset in CONFIG RESETSTAT (already wired in runtime)

## Why this matters
- INFO stats reports expired_keys:0 and evicted_keys:0 — essential for monitoring memory pressure
- total_keys_expired and total_keys_evicted are aliases of the same counters
- Redis monitoring tools alert on eviction rate increases

## Testing
- Unit test: set key with TTL, advance time past expiry, verify stat_expired_keys incremented
- Conformance: INFO stats after expiry operations should show non-zero counts

## Files to modify
- crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs (Store struct + drop_if_expired)
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs (active_expire_cycle, eviction_cycle)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:17:51.722909599Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:49:58.340538240Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T02:49:58.340200992Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankenredis-jkk","title":"Fix NOMKSTREAM behavior for existing empty streams","description":"## Problem

fr-command/src/lib.rs lines 4000-4007: XADD with NOMKSTREAM incorrectly returns nil for existing empty streams (created via XGROUP CREATE ... MKSTREAM).

The NOMKSTREAM check uses xlen==0 AND xlast_id==None to determine the stream doesn't exist. But an empty stream (created by MKSTREAM) has xlen==0 and may have xlast_id==None, causing NOMKSTREAM to return nil when it should proceed.

## What Redis Does

NOMKSTREAM only prevents creating a NEW stream key. If the key already exists as a stream (even empty), XADD proceeds normally.

## Fix

Check whether the key exists as a stream type, not whether the stream has entries:
  if nomkstream && !store.key_exists_as_type(key, \"stream\") {
      return Ok(RespFrame::BulkString(None));
  }

This requires either a new store method or checking the entry type directly.

## Files to Modify

- crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs: XADD NOMKSTREAM check
- crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs: May need a type-checking helper","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:10:46.076005101Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:34:50.649549505Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:34:50.647806736Z","close_reason":"Fixed: NOMKSTREAM now uses store.exists() to check key existence, not xlen/xlast_id","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankenredis-jzs","title":"Centralize version string as single const across all crates","description":"## Problem

The Redis version string appears in multiple locations:
- fr-command/src/lib.rs: \"redis_version:7.2.0-frankenredis\" (INFO)
- fr-runtime/src/lib.rs: hello_bulk(\"7.2.0\") (HELLO response)
- fr-runtime/src/lib.rs: (\"redis-ver\", \"7.2.0\") (RDB aux field — was 7.0.0, fixed to 7.2.0 in f8c5a8a)

While these are now consistent (all 7.2.0), there's no single source of truth. If the version needs to change, all three locations must be found and updated.

## Fix

1. Define a const in fr-runtime (or a shared crate): pub const REDIS_COMPAT_VERSION: &str = \"7.2.0\";
2. Use this const in all locations
3. Consider also deriving the FrankenRedis version from Cargo.toml via env!(\"CARGO_PKG_VERSION\")

## Files to Modify

- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs: Define const, use in RDB and HELLO
- crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs: Use const in INFO","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:11:03.925020086Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:34:50.855981956Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:34:50.854614367Z","close_reason":"Centralized REDIS_COMPAT_VERSION const in fr-store, used by fr-command INFO and fr-runtime HELLO/RDB","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankenredis-yys0","title":"Precompute ACL category->command mapping in OnceLock","description":"# Precompute ACL Category→Command Mapping in OnceLock

## What

command_categories() in crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:62 and
commands_in_acl_category() in crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:8924 recompute
ACL category→command mappings from scratch on every call. Each call iterates
21 ACL categories × the entire COMMAND_TABLE (~200 commands) and does
split_whitespace().collect::<Vec<&str>>() on each command's flags string —
~4,200 Vec allocations per invocation.

After the April 9 short-circuit (frankenredis-zjii), the DEFAULT user (no
category rules) never hits this code path. But any user with ACL category
rules (e.g., +@read -@dangerous) still pays the full cost on every command.

## Why This Matters

Production Redis deployments commonly use ACL categories for least-privilege
security. A web app might configure: user webuser +@read +@write -@dangerous.
That user has non-empty allowed_categories, so the short-circuit doesn't apply,
and every command pays ~4,200 allocations for category resolution.

## Design

Use OnceLock<HashMap<&'static str, Vec<&'static str>>> to build the mapping
once at startup:

```rust
static ACL_CATEGORY_MAP: OnceLock<HashMap<&'static str, Vec<&'static str>>> = OnceLock::new();

fn commands_in_acl_category_cached(category: &str) -> &'static [&'static str] {
    let map = ACL_CATEGORY_MAP.get_or_init(|| {
        let mut m = HashMap::new();
        for &cat in ACL_CATEGORIES {
            m.insert(cat, commands_in_acl_category_uncached(cat));
        }
        m
    });
    map.get(category).map(|v| v.as_slice()).unwrap_or(&[])
}
```

Also precompute the reverse mapping (command→categories) so
command_categories() is a single HashMap lookup:

```rust
static COMMAND_CATEGORY_MAP: OnceLock<HashMap<&'static str, Vec<&'static str>>> = OnceLock::new();
```

## Acceptance Criteria

1. commands_in_acl_category uses OnceLock-cached mapping
2. command_categories uses OnceLock-cached reverse mapping
3. Zero per-command allocations for ACL category resolution
4. All ACL conformance tests pass
5. Users with non-trivial ACL rules see no throughput regression vs default user
6. Memory overhead is bounded (21 categories × 200 commands = ~4200 pointers = ~33KB)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:29:12.934372724Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-10T21:39:17.081568068Z","closed_at":"2026-04-10T21:39:17.081295221Z","close_reason":"Cached ACL category mappings + command reverse map","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
